items.rationale: House's strongest conviction by late 2024: fees fell below a penny on L2s, unlocking cheap global/cross-border payments and a real merchant value prop. Repeatedly called the 'killer app' that has found product-market fit.
items.age_note: emerged 2023, intensified to top conviction by Q3-Q4 2024

items.rationale: Viewed as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a democratizing developer primitive. Jolt zkVM claimed fastest-in-world; team sees prover overhead falling enough to unlock many new applications. Also framed as a security necessity (handwritten circuits are buggy).
items.age_note: building since 2023 (Lasso/Jolt), peak excitement 2024

items.rationale: Pitched as the future of consumer crypto and a sector that needs decentralization, not just wants it. Backed Daylight (distributed energy). Defined as crypto networks forming consensus on physical state that can't be computationally verified.
items.age_note: interest from 2023, concretized with energy thesis/investments 2024

items.rationale: Two-sided thesis: crypto as counterweight to centralized AI, and crypto rails (provenance proofs, IP layers, agent wallets) as the coordination layer for an explosion of AI content. Strong interest in proof-of-authenticity and decentralized curation.
items.age_note: thesis from 2023 (Gensyn), broadened through 2024

items.rationale: Rollup-centric roadmap embraced as Ethereum's scaling endgame, but fragmentation of liquidity/state is the recognized open problem. Excited about shared sequencing, real-time proofs, and non-native DA to unify the ecosystem (Espresso, T1).
items.age_note: ongoing; interop emphasis sharpened in 2024

items.rationale: Long-standing market-design fascination meeting infrastructure: a programmable IP/licensing layer to let creators and AI co-exist with attribution and compensation. Led Story Protocol's $80M Series B — described as the most exciting infra innovation in 15 years.
items.age_note: NFT/IP theme from 2023, major commitment Aug 2024

items.rationale: Sees restaking as a two-sided market for raw cryptoeconomic security that could 100x innovation on consensus, VMs, oracles, bridges by unbundling security from the EVM. Led EigenLayer Series B.
items.age_note: high enthusiasm early-2024; later tempered by own restaking-risk research

items.rationale: Treated as a proven, growing core; interest in next-gen designs using ZK coprocessors for richer pricing/risk management at lower gas. Reported as still popular and growing in the Oct 2024 State of Crypto report.
items.age_note: persisted core; ZK-coprocessor angle is a 2024 addition

items.rationale: Recurring conviction that open social protocols fix web2 lock-in/holdup; energized by emerging developer ecosystems on Farcaster and the interoperability/portability they enable.
items.age_note: argued since 2023, active builder momentum late 2024

items.rationale: Held belief that crypto can't reach mainstream without privacy; excited by coSNARKs for private shared state, TEEs as underrated 'defense in depth,' and Aleo going live as private-yet-verifiable compute.
items.age_note: consistent stance 2023→2024

items.rationale: Most intense house concern: seen as setting the industry back by deterring serious founders, arming politicized regulators, and skewing public perception toward 'risky casino.' Distinguished from community tokens but viewed as predominantly net-negative.
items.age_note: casino critique from late 2023, peaked spring 2024

items.rationale: Repeatedly framed as an existential threat to innovation: easy/early liquidity (like SPACs) breeds get-rich-quick schemes and vaporware. Pushes 4yr/1yr-cliff lockups, dilutable token allocations, and milestone-tied unlocks; disclosure alone deemed insufficient.
items.age_note: consistent and intensifying through 2024

items.rationale: Dominant 2023-2024 grievance: SEC stretching Howey to secondary markets/exchanges/NFTs to create uncertainty and target chokepoints, deemed lawfare. Notably softened after the Nov 2024 election into constructive optimism about clearer rules.
items.age_note: hostile/defensive 2023-mid2024; shifted to cautious optimism Nov 2024 post-election

items.rationale: Flagged as an active, escalating threat being normalized in stages; positioned crypto as the credible counterbalance to financial-intermediary consolidation.
items.age_note: surfaced as named concern late 2024

items.rationale: Research-grounded worry that PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes block building via order-flow advantage ('big get bigger'); multi-block and cross-chain MEV make it worse. Also stake-weighted QoS and sandwiching concerns.
items.age_note: MEV concern from 2023, builder-centralization formalized Oct 2024

items.rationale: Tempering its own EigenLayer enthusiasm with formal analysis of when validators can be safely reused — worried a small slashing shock could cascade into catastrophic attack absent robust security conditions.
items.age_note: caveat published mid-2024, post-investment

items.rationale: A Nov 2024 California ruling that mere DAO participation (even forum posts) can trigger general-partnership liability is seen as a serious threat able to hold DAOs hostage; response is legal wrappers like DUNA.
items.age_note: acute new concern Nov 2024

items.rationale: Research-driven concern that stress triggers flight-to-safety and liquidity withdrawal precisely when needed, amplifying de-peg risk; winner-take-all stablecoin regulation/CBDCs seen as undermining decentralization.
items.age_note: detailed 2023; concentration worry restated Feb 2024, not heavily revisited since

items.rationale: Treated as the canonical sufficiently-decentralized, non-security asset; ETF approvals seen as enshrining 'ETH is not a security.' Most of the team expects ETH to be deflationary long-term, supporting per-coin value.
items.age_note: consistent bullishness; 'not a security' view reinforced through 2024

items.rationale: Respected as digital gold and the most successful internet-native 'religion,' though the team flags long-run security-budget risk as block rewards decline and is lukewarm on prioritizing a strategic BTC reserve.
items.age_note: store-of-value framing steady; reserve-priority skepticism Nov 2024

items.rationale: Considered legally 'okay' (issuer ≠ yield operator), transparent, and the leading rail behind the stablecoin PMF story; sub-cent transfers on L2s are central to the payments thesis.
items.age_note: legal comfort stated 2023, payments momentum 2024

items.rationale: Acknowledged as top chain by active addresses and DEX volume — but the team explicitly discounts active-address counts (low fees + gaming/incentive gaming) and flags sandwiching/MEV on Solana, so enthusiasm is qualified.
items.age_note: usage leadership noted 2024, but metric-skeptic and MEV caveats same year

items.rationale: The archetypal value-extractive instrument the house warns against — purely speculative, technically uninteresting, and damaging to adoption, regulation, and builder behavior. Distinguished narrowly from utility/community tokens.
items.age_note: sustained 2024 critique

items.rationale: Singled out for reserve-quality opacity: during stress markets fled toward more transparent coins (USDC/DAI) on peg/redemption fears, though USDT also benefits as a regulatory-arbitrage haven.
items.age_note: transparency concern detailed Sept 2023, not strongly restated since

items.rationale: Held up as the cautionary collapse — an algorithmic design plus an issuer-operated 20% yield ('bullshit') that the SEC straightforwardly won on. Regulators and the team align against algorithmic stablecoins.
items.age_note: post-mortem stance; asset defunct, cited as enduring lesson

risk_regime.rationale: As of 2024-11-30 the house is constructively risk-on but quality-selective. Hard metrics are at all-time highs (State of Crypto index, active addresses, DEX volume, stablecoin usage, mobile wallets), infra is 'snapping into focus' (cheap L2s, ZK, restaking), and the post-election turn produced explicit optimism that clearer U.S. rules will let well-meaning projects take off. Crucially, the bullishness is directed at productive building — stablecoins, ZK, DePIN, AI×crypto, IP — and paired with loud aversion to the 'casino' (memecoins, fast liquidity, short lockups) and near-term caution on enforcement and debanking. So: risk-on on fundamentals/builders, risk-off on speculation.

notes: Corpus is multi-voice (separate investors/researchers: a legal/policy voice on regulation & token structure, a cryptography voice on ZK/Jolt, a consumer/networks voice on apps & DePIN, an academic-transcript voice on MEV/consensus/stablecoins). 'Consensus' here means the dominant, repeated house positions; I weighted late-2024 statements heaviest. Strongest unifying thread across all voices: decentralization is the core value driver and the dividing line between productive crypto and 'web2 with extra steps' — restated as recently as 2024-11-27 ('Decentralization still matters'). Notable recency-driven reversal: regulatory posture flipped from adversarial/defensive (2023-mid2024) to cautiously optimistic after the Nov 2024 U.S. election, while still bracing for a final wave of enforcement. EigenLayer/restaking is an internal tension — strong investment-driven excitement (Feb 2024) tempered by the firm's own risk research (Aug 2024). Metric skepticism is itself a consensus view: the team distrusts 'active addresses' and estimates only 30-60M real monthly users (5-10% of owners), framing the owner→user conversion gap as the central growth opportunity. Older single-stated views (e.g., stablecoin run-risk mechanics, grassroots cryptocurrencies, quantum threat to signatures) are treated as persisted/background rather than active house theses.
